# Debugging runbook

Begin with the exact failure line, test name, stack path, and user constraint. Route from the detected path through indexed imports and symbols. For a patient service failure, inspect the focused test and service before the page or presentation component. For an authorization failure, inspect the tenant helper and retain the rejection behavior. For a missing audit event, inspect both the service call site and audit helper while preserving the public action name.

Compression keeps exact error messages and file references while removing repeated framework output. A compact result is useful only when it provides a credible first read and names any uncertainty. If no indexed path or symbol is detected, confidence remains low and the agent performs a targeted raw read. Broad logs can be revisited after the compact hypothesis is checked, but they are not the default first step.

Recommended commands should be focused and reproducible. The patient service test is preferred over a speculative whole-repository command when its path is known. After the focused test passes, the full suite catches adjacent regressions. This fixture does not execute an autonomous repair agent and therefore does not claim patch correctness. It measures deterministic preservation, routing, and context estimates from real core functions against known local evidence.
